symptoms: No LEDs light when wall power is applied to the barrel jack.
No rail activity anywhere — board appears completely dead.

likely_causes.mechanism: input fuse blown open after over-current event — barrel-jack rail never reaches R49
cracked solder fillet on barrel-jack pins from mechanical stress, intermittent VIN
input sense resistor burnt open after surge, breaking the VIN sense path

diagnostic_steps.action: DMM-probe the input side of R49 with wall PSU connected, referenced to GND
diagnostic_steps.expected: 9–32 V (24 V nominal); 0 V means VIN never reaches R49

diagnostic_steps.action: Continuity / resistance test across F1 in-circuit (power off)
diagnostic_steps.expected: <1 Ω closed; OL or kΩ = blown — replace SMD fuse with same rating

diagnostic_steps.action: Inspect J1 barrel-jack solder fillets under microscope for hairline cracks; reflow with flux if any pin is cracked
If F1 and J1 are good but R49 input side is 0 V, diode-mode R49 across its pads — open R49 has no continuity, replace same-value sense resistor
diagnostic_steps.expected: few-ohm reading typical for sense resistor; OL = burnt, replace

symptoms: White status LED D11 stays dark even with wall power applied.
LPC System Controller does not respond to any UART command on J22.

likely_causes.mechanism: always-on 3.3 V regulator output shorted to GND through damaged die — kills LPC_VCC rail
always-on 3.3 V regulator blown open — no LPC_VCC at output, LPC starved
LPC11U24 internal short on VDD pin loading LPC_VCC to GND through the System Controller die

diagnostic_steps.action: Probe J22 pin 15 for LPC_VCC referenced to GND
diagnostic_steps.expected: 3.3 V ± 5 %; 0 V means rail is missing

diagnostic_steps.action: With board off, diode-mode from U14 output pin to GND
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.3 / OL; <0.05 indicates short downstream of U14

diagnostic_steps.action: If U14 output reads short, lift U18 VDD pin (or hot-air remove U18) to isolate the load — re-measure diode value at U14 output
diagnostic_steps.expected: rail recovers (>0.3) → U18 was the loader; still shorted → U14 itself or another LPC_VCC consumer

diagnostic_steps.action: Replace U14 with a known-good donor part — hot air 350 °C, preheat board 130 °C, flux + fine tweezers
diagnostic_steps.expected: after rework, J22 pin 15 reads 3.3 V and D11 illuminates

symptoms: LPC responds on UART and D11 is lit at idle.
After sending the `1P` power-on command, D11 extinguishes instantly and the board shuts back down.

likely_causes.mechanism: SN65DSI86 die short on VDD12/VDD33 — pulls switched 3.3 V or 1.2 V rail to GND, sequencer trips
TUSB8041 internal short on VBUS / VDD12 — loads USB_PWR or VCC_1V2 hard at enable
level-shifter VCCA/VCCB internal short bridging 3V3_PWR_AUX to VCC_1V8 / GND
level-shifter VCCA/VCCB internal short bridging 3V3_PWR_AUX to VCC_1V8 / GND

diagnostic_steps.action: Issue `1P` while watching D11 (3.3 V switched indicator) and D12 (5 V indicator) — note which LED dies first
diagnostic_steps.expected: D11 dies first → 3V3_PWR_AUX shorted; D12 never lights → USB_PWR / 5 V path shorted

diagnostic_steps.action: Diode-mode the 3V3_PWR_AUX decoupling caps at U10, U28, U8 VDD pins to GND
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.3 / OL on each; <0.05 marks the loaded chip

diagnostic_steps.action: Diode-mode C37 (VCC_1V2) and the USB_PWR cap near D12 to GND
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.3 / OL; <0.05 = short on that rail, suspect U9 / U10

diagnostic_steps.action: Inject 1 V at 1 A on the shorted rail from a current-limited bench supply and locate the hot package by finger / thermal camera; hot-air remove the offending IC and verify rail recovers
diagnostic_steps.expected: rail returns to >0.3 in diode mode after the bad IC is removed

symptoms: Board powers up, LPC_VCC and 3V3_PWR_AUX are confirmed live, but SD-card boot always fails.
SD interface dead even with a known-good card.

likely_causes.mechanism: leaky / cracked MLCC on VCC_1V8 shorting the rail to GND — buck regulator hiccups, 1.8 V never appears
level shifter dead — VCC_1V8 present but no level translation between SOM and SD bus
level shifter dead — VCC_1V8 present but no level translation between SOM and SD bus

diagnostic_steps.action: Probe C130 with DMM for VCC_1V8
diagnostic_steps.expected: 1.8 V ± 5 %; 0 V = rail dead, oscillating = buck hiccup from short

diagnostic_steps.action: Power off, diode-mode C130 to GND
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.3 / OL; <0.05 = MLCC or downstream short — walk caps along the rail and inject 1 V/1 A to find the warm one

diagnostic_steps.action: If VCC_1V8 reads 1.8 V, probe both VCCA (3.3 V) and VCCB (1.8 V) pins of U28 and U8
diagnostic_steps.expected: both rails present at each shifter; missing rail means the shifter is starved, present rails + dead bus means the shifter itself is bad

diagnostic_steps.action: Replace the shorted MLCC (C130 or sister cap on rail) or hot-air the dead level shifter; reflow with flux, 350 °C
diagnostic_steps.expected: after rework, VCC_1V8 = 1.8 V at C130 and SD interface enumerates

symptoms: Internal display is dark and all USB ports are dead at the same time.
External HDMI output may still work.

likely_causes.mechanism: leaky MLCC shorting VCC_1V2 to GND — both U9 and U10 starve simultaneously
TUSB8041 internal die short on VDD12 — clamps VCC_1V2, hub never enumerates
SN65DSI86 internal die short on VCC1V2 — clamps the same rail, eDP bridge dead

diagnostic_steps.action: Probe C37 for VCC_1V2 with the board powered
diagnostic_steps.expected: 1.2 V ± 5 %; 0 V or oscillating = rail down

diagnostic_steps.action: Power off, diode-mode C37 to GND
diagnostic_steps.expected: >0.3 / OL; <0.05 = short on rail

diagnostic_steps.action: Hot-air remove U9 first and re-measure C37 diode value; if rail recovers, U9 was the loader. Otherwise repeat by lifting U10
diagnostic_steps.expected: rail returns to >0.3 once the shorted IC is off the board

diagnostic_steps.action: Replace the shorted IC (U9 hot air 360 °C / U10 BGA hot air 380 °C with 150 °C preheat) or the cracked MLCC; verify VCC_1V2 = 1.2 V and that 3V3_PWR_AUX and USB_PWR are still good
diagnostic_steps.expected: after rework, internal display lights and USB enumerates

symptoms: Internal display flickers or blanks intermittently, especially after reassembly or moving the lid.

likely_causes.mechanism: lifted ZIF contact / damaged gold flash on the 30-pin 0.5 mm DSI flex connector — intermittent contact on DSI lanes
cold joint under SN65DSI86 BGA on a DSI / eDP ball — signal drops when board flexes

diagnostic_steps.action: Inspect J24 ZIF contacts under microscope at ≥40×, looking for lifted gold fingers, bent contacts, or solder bridging at the SMT tail
Reflow J24 SMT tail with flux and a fine-tip iron at 320 °C; re-seat the flex with the actuator fully closed, then re-test
diagnostic_steps.expected: display stable across lid-flex test

diagnostic_steps.action: If a J24 pad is torn, scrape coverlay back to the trace stub and run a 38 AWG enamel jumper from the stub to the connector pin
diagnostic_steps.expected: continuity (<1 Ω) from connector pin to nearest via on the same net

diagnostic_steps.action: If J24 looks pristine, reflow U10 with hot air 350 °C and 130 °C bottom preheat to re-melt suspect BGA balls
diagnostic_steps.expected: intermittent flicker resolved after reflow

symptoms: microHDMI port feels physically loose or has separated from the PCB.
External display goes black or works only when the cable is held at a specific angle.

likely_causes.mechanism: pad lift / torn traces under the connector body from repeated plug-unplug mechanical stress

diagnostic_steps.action: Inspect the microHDMI footprint under microscope at ≥40×; identify which pads are still bonded vs. which have lifted with the connector
Wick old solder from remaining pads, clean with flux + IPA, then re-tin pads and the connector legs
diagnostic_steps.expected: shiny tinned pads, no oxide

diagnostic_steps.action: Re-solder the connector with a fine-tip iron at 350 °C; for any torn trace, scrape the coverlay ~2 mm back to expose the trace stub and bridge with 30 AWG enamel jumper to the connector pin
diagnostic_steps.expected: continuity (<1 Ω) verified pin-to-trace-stub on every signal pin

diagnostic_steps.action: Reinforce with UV-cure resin or epoxy fillet between the connector housing and PCB to absorb future mechanical stress

symptoms: On a v3.0 motherboard, the 5 V or 3.3 V main rail measures incorrectly (low, high, or oscillating) at the D12 / D11 indicators.
Board behaviour is unstable or hiccups during power-up.

likely_causes.mechanism: cold joint on the LM62460 thermal pad or inductor pin — bad return path destabilises the buck loop
feedback divider resistor cracked / wrong value — regulator targets the wrong setpoint, output is too low or too high
cracked output MLCC reduces output capacitance — buck oscillates on the feedback pin

diagnostic_steps.action: DMM-probe D11 pad for 3.3 V and D12 pad for 5 V — record exact value
diagnostic_steps.expected: 3.3 V ± 5 % and 5.0 V ± 5 %; outside band confirms rail issue

diagnostic_steps.action: Scope the LM62460 FB pin for clean DC (within a few mV of the internal reference)
diagnostic_steps.expected: stable DC; ripple > tens of mV or oscillation = unstable loop

diagnostic_steps.action: Inspect the LM62460 power-pad and inductor leads under microscope for cold joints, bridges, or fractured fillets — reflow with flux and hot air at 360 °C if any are suspect
If output cap looks cracked or rail oscillates, replace the output MLCC with the same value; verify rail returns to nominal at D11/D12
diagnostic_steps.expected: rail stabilises at 3.3 V or 5 V ± 5 % after rework
